meaning: n.刺耳的音调,不协和音,杂音
examples: All around was a cacophony of voices.
The resulting cacophony has drowned out everything else.
The drivers behind him honked, and the cacophony grew louder.
For companies looking for the exits, being heard above the cacophony can be a challenge.
He dashes through the lunchtime cacophony of taxis, camera swinging precariously by its strap.
